name: Aloe Vera
description: A succulent plant whose inner leaf gel contains acemannan polysaccharides that accelerate wound healing through macrophage activation — the definitive natural treatment for minor burns and wound care.
chemicalName: Aloe barbadensis Miller; acemannan (primary active polysaccharide)
molecularWeight: Variable (gel is ~99% water with dissolved polysaccharides)
appearance: Fresh gel: clear, viscous, slightly yellowish. Commercial: clear to slightly opaque.
chemistry: Acemannan activates macrophages via mannose receptors → IL-1, IL-6, TNF-α release → fibroblast proliferation → collagen synthesis. Bradykinase breaks down inflammatory bradykinin. Salicylic acid provides analgesic effect. Systematic review: reduces burn healing by ~9 days.
warnings: Aloe LATEX (yellow sap) contains aloin — potent laxative, IARC Group 2B carcinogen. Drain latex completely before harvesting gel. Fresh gel spoils in 5–7 days. Not for deep wounds or severe burns.
costNote: $4–8 per plant; $8–14 for 12 oz commercial gel
shelfLife: 5–7 days (fresh, refrigerated); months frozen; 1–2 years (commercial preserved)
